programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: First in a two-part story which sees emergency staff members involved in a devastating train crash. Meanwhile, a passenger struggles to keep calm as the full horror of her predicament becomes clear

As the hours drag on, tempers wear thin and a few old scores are settled before the crowing cock heralds the dawn. Festive special of the Highland medical drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen, Jessica Turner and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: A hypochondriac becomes the bane of Joanna's life, wasting time and resources with a string of fictitious illnesses, while Andrew attempts to help a girl's parents come to terms with her deafness. Medical drama, starring Gary Mavers and Haydn Gwynne

programme.desc: Hestor and William find the move to France quite an ordeal as she misplaces their passports and he forgets to drive on the right. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.desc: Hollywood beckons as Tom and Diana put on their directors' hats and make a promotional video, foiling Harvey's devious schemes in the process. Stephanie Cole, Graham Crowden and Daniel Hill star

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: British troops save Edith and Rene from execution, and the pair are released into the woods. Meanwhile, the Germans decide to head for Spain, and Michelle joins Crabtree in a duet. Classic wartime sitcom, starring Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy seems to have gained a stalker, but soon realises he has greater worries after discovering he promised to demonstrate archery at the vicar's garden party - while riding a horse. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Keith Clifford

programme.desc: The amateur sleuth is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers while holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London - a case involving a cleric whose disappearance is seemingly linked to an Irish train robbery. Feature-length mystery, starring Joan Hickson, Caroline Blakiston and Joan Greenwood

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and her seasoned colleagues reopen the case of a political aide's murder when a dormant offshore bank account containing £40,000 is discovered in his name. Ben Ransley was killed in 1997 on the evening of Labour's landslide victory and in his wallet was a photo of a beautiful blonde. But neither his mother nor his best friend was able to identify the mystery woman. Is she the key to the crime? Drama, with former Brookside star John McArdle (Billy Corkhill), Jason Durr of Heartbeat fame and one-time Bad Girls actress Kika Mirylees joining Denis Lawson, Dennis Waterman and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The agents observe jailed Algerian terrorist Malik, whom they suspect of planning an attack from his cell. In a risky move, he is released so MI5 can track him to his movement's headquarters - but the team's suspicions about him are incorrect. Jimi Mistry and Joanna Roth guest star in the high-octane drama, with Rupert Penry-Jones and Peter Firth

With Andy Serkis, Ray Winstone and Geraldine James

Discovering he intends to exact his revenge, she is not prepared to give in without a fight. Drama, starring Olga Sosnovska

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey investigates an assault on a prostitute and ends up offering her a room for the night, ignoring Jack's advice to avoid emotional involvement. Over at the Beeches, Joanna attends to some of the injured, but is terrified of risking her unborn baby's health by inhaling the toxic fumes. Rural drama, starring Gary Mavers, Joseph Millson and Susannah Corbett

programme.desc: Rene is imprisoned by communist leader Louise, who plans to use him as her love slave, while Mimi, Edith and Yvette are convinced he is dead and decide to hold a seance

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Kenneth Branagh returns to his Bafta-winning role of the Swedish detective in three adaptations of stories by Henning Mankell. Wallander moves into a new home with his girlfriend, only for their dreams of domestic bliss to be shattered when they discover a skeleton in the garden. He must find the killer if he has any hopes of getting his life settled again - and is surprised to discover the body was buried more recently than he first thought. Saskia Reeves and Sarah Smart co-star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: After Sandra's departure, the detectives are anxious to meet their new boss, and DCI Sasha Miller wastes no time getting cracking with her first Ucos case, the murder of an Italian immigrant on a west London allotment 25 years earlier. The ritualistic style of the killing puts one of the other gardeners in the frame when it's revealed he is a practising druid. However, as the investigation continues, Gerry becomes suspicious of Sasha - why is she kissing a senior officer? Tamzin Outhwaite joins the cast as DCI Miller, with Dennis Waterman, Denis Lawson and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The agents find themselves forced to rely on a British traitor from the Cold War era when they infiltrate a Russian firm suspected of stealing secrets from GCHQ. Struggling to cope with recent events, Adam insists on running the operation - but it is soon clear he has taken on too much. George Baker (Inspector Wexford) guest stars in the high-octane drama, with Rupert Penry-Jones and Peter Firth

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of properties in St Helens in Merseyside, London's Dulwich and the Derbyshire village of Loscoe

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The officers go undercover to stop retailers selling acid to teenagers, and track down the crooked mother and daughter letting agents who are pocketing tenant's money

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Aberdeen, Sarah and Stewart are called to help a lady who has fallen at home, while in Glasgow, crew members rush to a woman struggling with breathing problems

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: As part of Bargain Hunt's 20th anniversary celebrations, Christina Trevanion looks back at some of the best items bought by teams over the past two decades

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a wirelessly-charged LED flashlight, a sustainable wash bag, and stylish, waterproof scarf

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple from Surrey town to find a new home in the Welsh Borders, and also goes behind the scenes with conservation work at the Slimbridge Wetland Centre

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Witley, Surrey, Sarah Moore calls on blacksmith Kev Paxton to revamp a pair of metal bed ends, while upholsterer Ray Clarke gives an old armchair a complete redesign. For her own transformation, Sarah has a chest of drawers carcass to repurpose

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including a carved temple statue, a block of silver and a penny farthing. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones and Gethin Jones

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, David and Harry compete to design an eco-friendly garden in Whitchurch, Hampshire, which includes a space for entertaining and tackles a dangerous two-metre drop

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a threadbare theatre chair from Derby Hippodrome, a rare timepiece that has not worked for years, a cherished bronze locket and two First World War flying mascots

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: The Friday night shift takes its toll on the ambulance service crews in the capital as they deal with reports of an armed male and two stabbings. Meanwhile, Adam and Orani are dispatched to an elderly man who is seeing things, while Celia tries to get an ambulance to a patient having a nervous breakdown for over four hours

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: The girls head upstate to celebrate Mallory's engagement. At the cabin, Darby struggles to get through to Sara as their paths continue to diverge. Starring Anna Kendrick, Zoe Chao and Sasha Compere

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: After a serendipitous reunion, Darby and Augie find themselves falling into a comfortable, full-blown relationship once more. Starring Anna Kendrick and Jin Ha

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: As she juggles an overwhelming amount of adult responsibilities, Darby enjoys a rare night to herself. Starring Anna Kendrick and Zoe Chao

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Laurence asks Kris to fight in a charity boxing match to raise cash for a homeless shelter. Can he make the leap from mild-mannered hotel manager to prizefighter? VIP guest Louise Brookes checks in, asking for the same room she stayed in 175 times while she attended the inquest into the Hillsborough disaster, and Ciara launches a new competition to promote weddings at the hotel

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford investigate why energy bills are leaving customers at their wits' end, and fraud on a big-name internet shopping site

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Cumbria, Pembrokeshire and Derbyshire, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement agents shut down a filthy takeaway infested with mice, and investigate a salmonella outbreak that struck down more than 50 guests at a wedding. Narrated by Harry Hepple

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Near Peterhead, an elderly patient who has difficulty moving needs to be transferred to hospital. Ambulance crew Ricky and Luan use their skills to help him into the ambulance

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: To celebrate 20 years of the programme, Eric Knowles takes a look back at some of the best and worst buys the reds and blues have bought over two decades

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers test and score a dog-training tool, vegan nail varnish and a T-shirt for children that can be coloured-in, while the makers watch on. Presented by Lucy Alexander

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple looking for a home in the Wye Valley, and also examines the challenges of sustainably sourcing and restocking the European eel in the River Wye

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, including a dressing table and mirror, and vintage suitcase

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage table hockey game, an early-20th-century glass chandelier and antique sketch books that belonged to the artist Henry Thomson

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Gethin Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Two siblings seek dietary advice for their parents, who have let healthy eating fall by the wayside after a stressful year dealing with bereavement and caring for an elderly mother. Gregg Wallace demonstrates that a satisfying meal can be made easily from fresh ingredients without their frequently used cooking sauces, while Chris Bavin suggests healthy homemade alternatives to takeaways

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at memorable advertising tasks - a staple of the show and famously Lord Sugar's favourite of all the assignments. There's a chance to relive classic clangers such as Jet Pop - the most inappropriately named airline in history, and Elizabeth making a modern car advert in a medieval village. Plus, a lesson in exactly how it should have been done when Carina used her loaf to make a bread commercial. Watching along are Lord Sugar, Baroness Brady and Claude Littner. They'll reveal the ads worth tuning in for, and those that had them switching off. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Fergus Walsh discuss the latest on the Covid-19 crisis

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With double Olympic taekwondo champion Jade Jones, England rugby union winger Ugo Monye, gold medal-winning hockey star Sam Quek and former Manchester United defender Gary Pallister. Sue Barker asks the questions, with team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves old tents, four dining chairs and a bamboo dressing screen from being thrown in the tip, hoping she and two others can make money by restoring the items

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As autumn gets underway, Monty Don gives advice on what plants need to be taken under cover now, what can stay out until after the first frosts and others that just need an extra layer of protection. Plus, he shows what can be planted now in the vegetable garden. Joe Swift visits Good Morning Britain presenter Kate Garraway in her London garden and discusses how it has been a sanctuary over the months her husband has been seriously ill. The programme also returns to Nick Bailey's home to see the results of the design project he started in his own back garden earlier in the year and meets a Kent woman who has filled her garden with exotic and tropical plants

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Quiz show in which a team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques auctioneer Charles Hanson, trader Linda Lambert and luxury upcycler Zoe Pocock head to Cambridge to search for bargains, competing to make the most money

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Weld is over-run with holiday-makers and the locals no longer have the beach to themselves - and when Woody braves the ocean, a collision with a jet-ski puts him in a coma

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The quarter-finalists face British and Irish fabrics week. First up is the pattern challenge, which is a stern test of their soft tailoring skills, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask the sewers to create a 'Workers Jacket' from linen. Next, it's the transformation challenge, and the sewers are asked to take inspiration from the British seaside by turning stripy deckchairs and parasols into stylish garments in just 90 minutes. Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan travels across Ethiopia to find out how people can live so close to hyenas, powerful carnivores that weigh up to 14 stone and are capable of killing lions in packs. He starts his journey in the capital Addis Ababa, where hyenas prowl the outskirts for food and are tolerated because people believe they ward off the devil. He also meets cattle-herders who dislike hyenas because of attacks on their livestock, and visits the medieval city of Harar, which has a reputation for having hundreds of hyenas, many of which inhabit the local graveyard

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the historic venues from the series, as well as new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin uncovers the history of the border town Berwick-upon-Tweed

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mike Bushell, Aisling Bea, Sunetra Sarker and Dion Dublin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: During a trip, there is a celebration on board Westro as apprentice Stephen West turns 20 and officially receives his skipper's licence, while fishing boat Sardonyx is on the hunt for squid in the Moray Firth. In Peterhead, part-timer Arthur James McKenzie is following in his grandfather's footsteps fishing for lobsters just 50 metres from shore

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Al Murray and Paul Chowdhry join experts James Braxton and Natasha Raskin Sharp on the hunt for bargains to sell at auction. A classic Rolls Royce and an Aston Martin take the two comedians west of London and while Al picks up some antiques with a distinct military flavour, Paul discovers he's a 'hustler' when haggling for collectables and curios. Eventually the pair head to auction in Buckinghamshire

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The Fraserburgh crew race to save a stricken fishing boat without a working rudder in a force-eight gale. On Loch Ness, a yacht becomes trapped on a weir, and one wrong move by the lifeboat crew could send it over the edge. On the north Devon coast, the Minehead crew comes to the aid of a puppy that has swallowed a fishing hook

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A team of transplant surgeons perform a double lung transplant operation, a procedure that must be carried out against the clock to avoid tissue deterioration. They successfully remove one diseased lung and replace it with a healthy one. However, just as they cut off the blood supply to the second lung, the transplanted organ stops working. At Addenbrooke's, a neurosurgeon removes a bundle of abnormal blood vessels sitting near the top of a patient's spinal cord

programme.title: Out of Her Mind
programme.desc: Sitcom, written by and starring Sara Pascoe, exploring heartbreak, family and how to survive them. When her sister announces she is getting engaged, Sara responds by trying to prove scientifically that love does not exist. Scared that the people she cares about are outgrowing her, Sara tries to befriend an anti-social fast food employee

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan. Mandy Carter is a woman with big dreams - and today her dream is to find work. But the only thing available to her is a job in a fruit packing factory, where a big mallet will come in handy. With Michelle Greenidge and Tom Basden

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Enslaved with Samuel L Jackson
programme.desc: A look at those who resisted slavery, including the famous Underground Railroad and freedom boats that helped runaway slaves to escape to the safety of Canada, using a network of safe houses to make the perilous journey north. The programme also examines the influence of famous freedom fighter Harriet Tubman and the black soldiers who served in the Union Army during the Civil War

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Checking into the Shankly this week is a party of 41 stags, who are being looked after by trainee guest relations manager James. But the morning before the lads arrive, he is nowhere to be found. Owner Lawrence challenges staff to set up a glamping festival on the roof in just one week, a loved-up couple need a last-minute bridal suite and a bride-to-be brings her friends to the hotel for her second hen party this year

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian presents a guide to medical dramas, looking back at shows including Emergency Ward 10, Dr Finlay's Casebook, Casualty, Angels and Doctors. Harry assesses each show for the authenticity of sets and costumes, celebrates some of the most frequently recurring tropes of the genre, and reveals what it takes to make a successful hospital drama

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A village becomes the scene of a violent gunfight, firearms officers fear for their lives when confronted by an armed man, and two paramedics are victims of a terrifying attack

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues a drop-arm sofa, an old chest and a train set from Witley Recycling Centre, enlisting the help of restorer Peter Fournel and upcycler Jacqui Joseph

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Manchester single mothers Hayley and Megan join forces to help improve each other's diet, and turn to Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin for help in finding healthy meals all of their children will enjoy. Chris also visits a butcher to report on the importance of buying locally produced meat and asks members of the public what they think of some of the lesser known, cheaper cuts

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Auctioneer Catherine Southon, dealer Tony Wong and upholsterer Micaela Sharp scour the antique shops, scrapyards and flea markets of Faversham in search of items to turn a profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Almost a year has passed since the death of George's wife. When it turns out Shay has stolen Laura's ashes from her grandparents and brought them back to New Zealand, they set out to Weld to retrieve them

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts the semi-final, as the four remaining sewers take on garments and techniques from around the world. Firstly, they must follow a pattern like none they've encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a pair of Indian Dhoti Pants. Next is the Transformation Challenge, as the sewers take on a garment from West Africa, the Dashiki. For their final challenge, the Made to Measure, the semi-finalists take on the fiendishly tricky high-end Japanese couture technique of origami

programme.title: Mountain: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: The extraordinary animals and people who make their homes in mountain ranges around the world, beginning with the Rockies. The mountains are the spine of North America, a beautiful wilderness of snowcapped peaks and hidden valleys, home to many animals including cougars, wolves and grizzly bears

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team is in Old College, Aberystwyth University, on the west coast of Wales, where Christina Trevanion values a pair of Wedgwood plates, while a gold guinea impresses Raj Bisram

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mike Bushell, Aisling Bea, Sunetra Sarker and Dion Dublin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Goat's milk ice-cream producers Ian and Lauren and cider-makers Shawn and Trudie compete with the weather and the FIFA World Cup at the Great British Food Festival

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steps star Ian 'H' Watkins and actress and singer Claire Sweeney join experts James Braxton and David Harper on a quest to find items to sell at auction, with the friends scouring the Welsh Border for antiques. While H and James gamble a vast portion of their cash on two large items, Claire and David opt for toys and Victorian belt buckles

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite recipes for biscuits and small bites to snack on. She kicks things off with her deceptively simple but extremely beautiful Italian-inspired amaretti biscuits, before demonstrating how to make deep-fried chicken doughnuts. Finally, Nadiya shares her recipe for chocolate-dipped florentines

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the final episode, Nadiya wraps up the series with a selection of recipes for special occasions, including an eight-layered birthday cake, a sparkly Mardi Gras king cake, hot cross buns, and a spectacularly festive biscuit tower

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Return of the period drama set in London in the 1760s following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. Isaac and Hal Pincher have become the new owners of the Saracen's Head tavern, and have the ambition of taking over London bawdy houses. Meanwhile, Lucy tries to branch out on her own by joining forces with Elizabeth Harvey and her son Fredo, and Lydia Quigley continues to suffer the abandonment of her family

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: A light-hearted look at the social and cultural changes society went through since the turn of the 21st century, featuring contributions from politicians and celebrities. In this episode, Angela Scanlon, Ellie Taylor and Geoff Norcott take a light-hearted look back at the year 2000, from Kylie's iconic gold hotpants to Anne Robinson becoming TV's most feared woman

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Julia struggles to abide by the rules and clashes with Mrs Lamb as she attends her first-ever school sports day, where apparently it's no longer acceptable to cheer for individual children and spectators can only offer generic support like 'good job'. Over at the Kidiverse soft-play centre, Kevin is devastated that an important work presentation means he will miss the dads' race. Liz worries that her new boyfriend is blanking her, and Anne discovers something that leads to a nasty surprise for Amanda

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: The live-streaming of an attack on mosques in Christchurch, New Zealand, in March 2019 heralds a new era of terrorism and confirms the far right as a fast-growing threat. Survivor Abdul Aziz describes how he was praying when a fellow worshipper was shot through a window, and he ran outside to confront the attacker, while analyst Jacob Davey recalls monitoring the digital footprint of the attack

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Essex, the crew at Walton and Frinton gets an unusual mayday call to a ship called HMS Pickle. Arriving on scene, they discover it is not the modern naval vessel they expected, but a replica of a ship that once served in Nelson's fleet, and though the original survived the battle of Trafalgar, this one is shipping water and sinking fast. On the east coast of Scotland, the crew at Anstruther faces a tricky rescue trying to extract a walker with a badly broken leg from a remote coastal path. And in the Shannon basin in Ireland, the volunteers at one of the RNLI's few inland stations at Lough Derg are woken in the early hours of the morning by their pagers - cries for help have been heard coming from the 40-mile-long lake. After launching into inky darkness and thick fog, the crew make an eerie discovery

programme.title: The Shipman Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Chris Wilson examines how the idea of 'a good death' helped Harold Shipman escape detection. Since his victims died painlessly in the comfort of their own home, many family members were too grateful for the lack of suffering to question anything untoward about the doctor's actions. He talks to the family that led a successful campaign to reexamine every death connected to Shipman throughout his medical career - going back to 1974

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness return with the motoring magazine, beginning by heading to Paddy's home town of Bolton to test three new company cars by spending 24 hours in them - with no getting out. Plus, Chris heads to Italy to put the Ferrari SF90 supercar through its paces

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Two life-changing assaults cost a police officer his career and have a huge impact on his health as he is diagnosed with complex PTSD

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Witley, Surrey, Sarah Moore calls on blacksmith Kev Paxton to revamp a pair of metal bed ends, while upholsterer Ray Clarke gives an old armchair a complete redesign. For her own transformation, Sarah has a chest of drawers carcass to repurpose

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Three people who have never met before must work as a team and answer questions correctly in a bid to win a share of a £20,000 cash prize. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert Roo Irvine, trader Danny Sebastian and upcycling blacksmith Kevin Paxton head to the Scottish Borders to rummage through its antique shops, scrap yards and flea markets in the hope of finding items they can each turn into a healthy profit. When the day is done and they've got their items, they have just three weeks to turn them into cash

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: It is the annual cricket match between Weld and their sworn enemies Stafford, and George has been roped into the team as a ringer despite being banned from cricket in Australia

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contest, hosted by Joe Lycett, reaches its final as the last three contestants take on a trio of evening wear challenges before the winner is announced. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young begin by asking the sewers to create a double-breasted waistcoat. Next is the Transformation challenge, as the trio repurpose net curtains into exquisite, red carpet-ready, pieces of evening wear. Lastly, for the Made to Measure challenge, the sewers attempt to construct and fit glamorous strapless evening gowns for their models, this time hand-picked for friends and family

programme.title: Mountain: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: This episode looks at life on the highest mountain range on Earth, the Himalayas, home to many extraordinary animals and remarkable ancient cultures. The programme follows snow leopards as they creep into isolated mountain villages searching for food, and monkeys huddling for warmth in a hidden, frozen forest, and the athletes who compete in the gruelling Everest marathon

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Five experts assess items brought to the Blickling estate in Norfolk, with David Harper excited by an early device for watching films, while David Fletcher and James Lewis reminisce about Rupert the Bear books and Dinky toys. Paul Martin enjoys a ride in a tank on historic former military camp

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mike Bushell, Aisling Bea, Sunetra Sarker and Dion Dublin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Alpaca farmers Ian and Caroline and former circus ringmaster-turned-farmer Drew compete with their alpacas for coveted red rosettes at the Royal Three Counties Show

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary talks to award-winning film-maker and Turner Prize-winning artist Steve McQueen, discussing his views on growing up in London and his new BBC One anthology series Small Axe. Set from the late-1960s to the mid-1980s, each film tells a different story involving racism and discrimination in London's West Indian community

programme.title: Wild Shetland - Scotland's Viking Frontier
programme.desc: Ewan McGregor narrates a portrait of wildlife on the Shetland Islands, tracing the course of a breeding season from the depths of winter to high summer. Cameras follow puffins, diving gannets and storm petrels nesting in an ancient Pictish tower, a mother otter protecting her young twin cubs, and capture a pod of orcas hunting seals of the island's rocky coasts

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: Key players including Rudy Giuliani, Stormy Daniels, Steve Bannon and Ambassador John Bolton shed light on Trump's second year, as he faces challenges from his past

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Tom Allen, Angela Barnes, Rhys James, Nigel Ng and Athena Kugblenu. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Highlights from the satirical news show, presented by Nish Kumar, as a cast of comedy correspondents give their take on the week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A team of transplant surgeons perform a double lung transplant operation, a procedure that must be carried out against the clock to avoid tissue deterioration. They successfully remove one diseased lung and replace it with a healthy one. However, just as they cut off the blood supply to the second lung, the transplanted organ stops working. At Addenbrooke's, a neurosurgeon removes a bundle of abnormal blood vessels sitting near the top of a patient's spinal cord

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin revisits his travel series, beginning with Around the World in 80 Days. He draws on his personal archive of audio recordings and diary notebooks, and reflects on how he became a seasoned global traveller. The first episode revisits his visits to Venice, Cairo, Saudi Arabia, India, Hong Kong and China - a nation that was closed to most westerners at the time. Featuring contributions from fans and friends including David Attenborough, Joanna Lumley and Simon Reeve

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: Documentary following the stories of ordinary people fighting for truth and justice following acts of terror. The first one featured is a hostage crisis in Syria that culminated in seven beheadings by a British executioner, later dubbed Jihadi John, while the second looks at the far-right attack in Christchurch, New Zealand, in which 51 people were shot dead in the city's mosques

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A police officer shot at close range is in danger of losing his sight, and a speeding motorcyclist is pursued through London

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: An exploration of the textile mills and factories that once thrived in Britain during the Industrial Revolution. From New Lanark in Scotland to the mill town of Burnley, Fred meets ex-workers to discover what life was really like, and chats to a group that has dedicated 30 years to restoring mill engines

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Mountains seem to sing in harmony with the sky as Bob Ross' brushes tell a story in breathtaking hues and depth

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the history of the Norse warriors, delving into their customs and searching for the truth behind their fearsome reputation. He begins by telling the story of their origins, and investigates the world of their ancient ancestors. The programme charts Scandinavia's prehistoric landscape, from the Baltic Sea to Norway's Atlantic coast, and Neil examines archaeological evidence to create a portrait of the society from which the Vikings emerged

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: Neil Oliver presents the drama-documentary telling the tale of the bloody 17th century Civil War. When the Scottish Parliament, led by the Chief of Clan Campbell, declares war on Charles I, Clans loyal to the King rise up in rebellion. At the heart of this epic struggle lies an ancient feud between the Campbells and the MacDonalds, and the Royalist Clans use the war to wreak bloody vengeance on their rivals in an epic conflict that threatens the stability of the newly emerging British state

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: One-off drama by Jim Hawkins and Horace Ove, based on the events surrounding the Spaghetti House siege - an armed robbery that went disastrously wrong. Starring T-Bone Wilson, Trevor Thomas and Archie Pool. Originally broadcast in 1979

programme.title: I Am Not Your Negro (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary-maker Raoul Peck uses an unfinished manuscript by James Baldwin, an account of the lives and successive assassinations of civil rights leaders Medgar Evans, Malcolm X and Martin Luther King Jr, as the springboard for this examination of the black experience in America. Narrated by Samuel L Jackson, the film uses Baldwin's words as it explores everything from Hollywood stereotypes to police brutality

programme.title: Yellow Is Forbidden
programme.desc: Documentary following Chinese fashion designer Guo Pei as she aims to switch from dressing her country's elite to trying to conquer the world of haute couture in Paris

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Mountains seem to sing in harmony with the sky as Bob Ross' brushes tell a story in breathtaking hues and depth

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: An exploration of the textile mills and factories that once thrived in Britain during the Industrial Revolution. From New Lanark in Scotland to the mill town of Burnley, Fred meets ex-workers to discover what life was really like, and chats to a group that has dedicated 30 years to restoring mill engines

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: Neil Oliver presents the drama-documentary telling the tale of the bloody 17th century Civil War. When the Scottish Parliament, led by the Chief of Clan Campbell, declares war on Charles I, Clans loyal to the King rise up in rebellion. At the heart of this epic struggle lies an ancient feud between the Campbells and the MacDonalds, and the Royalist Clans use the war to wreak bloody vengeance on their rivals in an epic conflict that threatens the stability of the newly emerging British state

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: The former steeplejack examines the history of the iron and steel industries, beginning with a visit to Ironbridge Gorge in Shropshire. Fred moves on to the Black Country, where he learns how the anchor for the ill-fated Titanic was made, before getting the chance to drive the biggest steam-powered rolling mill engine he has ever seen

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he demonstrates how to paint emerald waters in beautiful green shades

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis explores how castles came under threat from the cannon during the Wars of the Roses, before their whole purpose was transformed in the Tudor era when some of the buildings became palaces to impress monarchs such as Elizabeth I. However, just as they seemed to have lost their defensive function the English Civil War erupted. Sam also looks at their legacy and how they remain popular to this day

programme.title: The Flying Scotsman: A Rail Romance
programme.desc: To celebrate its 90th birthday, Barbara Flynn narrates the story of the nation's love affair with the Flying Scotsman - arguably the most famous locomotive in the world, and one that symbolises all that was great about British engineering. In 2004, public donations and grants were used to purchase the train and keep it in Britain, where the National Railway Museum has been working to restore it to its former glory. First shown in 2013

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Between 1948 and 1960, governments around the world invested in electric tracks and diesel, but in Britain hundreds of steam-powered trains were built. This film looks at the reasons behind their continued production, and asks what happened to them

programme.title: A Very British History
programme.desc: Sue Brown looks at what life was like in the post-war years for Birmingham's Caribbean community. The poet, whose parents came to Britain in the 1950s from Jamaica, meets people who remember and experienced the hostile environment that faced many of the early immigrants. She also explores her home city to discover the cultural significance of local black churches, music and the rise of Rastafarianism, and meets singer Jimmy Cliff to discuss his hit song Many Rivers to Cross

programme.title: The Billion Dollar Art Hunt
programme.desc: John Wilson follows a tip-off that the world's biggest art theft from Boston's Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum in 1990, valued at a billion dollars, may be about to be found - not in America, but in Dublin

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Cameras follow Yinka Shonibare as he creates the latest in his series of distinctive mannequin-like figures, which are anonymous, headless and dressed in African batik fabrics. The artist also talks about his life and career, with his work being influenced by his Nigerian origins and combining darkness and humour to challenge ideas about cultural identity and the post-colonial world

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he demonstrates how to paint emerald waters in beautiful green shades

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: The former steeplejack examines the history of the iron and steel industries, beginning with a visit to Ironbridge Gorge in Shropshire. Fred moves on to the Black Country, where he learns how the anchor for the ill-fated Titanic was made, before getting the chance to drive the biggest steam-powered rolling mill engine he has ever seen

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis explores how castles came under threat from the cannon during the Wars of the Roses, before their whole purpose was transformed in the Tudor era when some of the buildings became palaces to impress monarchs such as Elizabeth I. However, just as they seemed to have lost their defensive function the English Civil War erupted. Sam also looks at their legacy and how they remain popular to this day

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: A tour of Britain's mining heritage takes Fred from Cornwall to Scotland, where he visits sites of lead, slate, tin and coal extraction. Along the way, the machine-mad former steeplejack has a chance to man a winding engine and descends a mine shaft while strapped to the top of the cage

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Snowy peaks point toward the heavens as American painter Bob Ross surrounds a scene in a happy gathering of landscape finery

programme.title: She Wore a Yellow Ribbon (1949)
programme.desc: A cavalry officer on the brink of retirement takes drastic steps to prevent an impending war with the Arapaho people, stirred up by double-dealing agents and Custer's recent defeat. After a series of skirmishes and minor victories, he decides the best solution is to ride into the Arapaho camp and negotiate a truce. John Ford Western, starring John Wayne, Joanne Dru, John Agar and Ben Johnson

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: How the landscapes of America's five great deserts challenged the pioneers' westward push. Beginning in Monument Valley on the Arizona-Utah state line, Ray discovers how the US cavalry struggled in the hot arid landscape, explores how the Apache adapted their warfare methods to the hostile conditions and reveals why the forbidding landscape proved the perfect refuge for bandits

programme.title: Glen Campbell: The Rhinestone Cowboy
programme.desc: An insight into the life of country music star Glen Campbell, documenting his remarkable journey from an impoverished childhood to worldwide fame and success as a guitarist and singer with records including Wichita Lineman and Rhinestone Cowboy. Featuring contributions by family, friends and colleagues including Jimmy Webb, Micky Dolenz and Bob Harris

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: Neil Oliver presents the drama-documentary telling the tale of the bloody 17th century Civil War. When the Scottish Parliament, led by the Chief of Clan Campbell, declares war on Charles I, Clans loyal to the King rise up in rebellion. At the heart of this epic struggle lies an ancient feud between the Campbells and the MacDonalds, and the Royalist Clans use the war to wreak bloody vengeance on their rivals in an epic conflict that threatens the stability of the newly emerging British state

programme.title: Big Sky, Big Dreams, Big Art: Made in the USA
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak focuses on the art of the new cities of the east coast of America, with their futuristic skylines and lofty skyscrapers. He explores the squalid boxing rings painted by George Bellows, Reginald Mash's decadent away days on Coney Island and the escape into theosophy and abstraction mounted by Thomas Wilfred. He ends by looking at the harsh immigrant experience of Ellis Island and the profound impact that rootlessness had on the art of Mark Rothko.

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a magic lampshade of sailing ships and a theatre of dancing grandfather clocks, and watch as Book Boy makes a bouncy castle.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: In the first episode we meet four-year-old Ottalie who lives on a farm. Today Ottalie helps out, collecting fresh eggs from the chickens, cleaning out their run, and giving them new straw. Back inside it's time for a game of hide-and-seek with Mum and little sister Imogen. But when Dad gets home, will he give away Mum's hiding place?

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Tig is given a bicycle for her birthday, but will not take advice from her mother on how to ride it

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: When Big Fish can't go on a holibob with the Olobobs, they bring their holiday to her!

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Marian's gyuveche is made using layers of peppers, tomatoes and feta with an egg on top

programme.title: Timmy Time

programme.desc: With her parents stay at the hospital on the mainland awaiting the delivery of the new baby, Katie looks after the family cat, Fabbydoo, but he mysteriously disappears

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: Mum and Melody visit Shoreham Lifeboat Station. Mum says she has some music for Melody to listen to called Storm Interlude from Peter Grimes, by Benjamin Britten

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: A makes friends with a cat, but it quickly becomes clear she is allergic to her new feline pal

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘key' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy wonders why it has to rain after a downpour washes away his prized sandcastle

programme.title: Something Special

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Dog Loves Books

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Rory visits Sebastian and his guinea pig Bonbon who has a lump on her leg that needs checking by a vet. Ferne goes to meet a vet who is caring for a badger at a wildlife hospital

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old Ottalie is visited by Grandad and Grandma on the farm. They help Ottalie pick apples from the tree, before Ottalie and Grandma get to work making a tasty apple sponge for pudding

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is sending a letter in the post to the CBeebies house. She finds out how posting a letter works as she follows the letter's journey from the post box to the sorting office and finally to the house. Using a special infrared light, Maddie shows us how stamps are scanned to tell if they are first or second class. Next, she visits a factory where colouring pencils are made. She finds out how the coloured crayon inside is made from clay and, using her slow-motion camera, we see the wooden shaft of the pencil being made.

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: It is a windy day on the Scarlet Squid, and Captain Captain is devastated when her pirate hat blows away

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: With a landslide about to fall into the valley, Peter and his friends realise that a hibernating Mrs Tiggy-Winkle is in the path of destruction

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It's Befriend a Bug Day, but Love Monster keeps accidentally annoying or scaring the bugs in his garden. When Elder Kitten reminds him that he is DJing at the Befriend a Bug party later, he starts practising his beatboxing. He notices some caterpillars on a nearby branch dancing to the music, but when he tries to approach them, they get frightened and curl up. Realising that they aren't afraid of him while he makes music, Love Monster builds them a miniature stage and happily performs for them. But when the party starts, they all appear to have vanished - or so it seems...

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies’ very own motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that help make roads! Grace helps operate a road planer, checks out a road printing machine and then has a go in a road paver!

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: The presenter travels 150 million years into the past to the time of the plant-eating brachiosaurus, and also tries to find a branch of a monkey puzzle tree

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bob can't go to his friend Phoebe's party because she has measles. This puts a stop to his aim to try and beat his previous record of shooting 4 basketball hoops in a row. Bitz decides to throw the best beach party ever to make up for it. There are snacks, games, a dance floor and even a basketball hoop! But it's a very warm day and soon everyone is too hot to party. Will they have to give up and call it a day or can Bitz find a way to cool everyone down so that Bob can try and beat his record?

programme.desc: A look back at memorable advertising tasks - a staple of the show and famously Lord Sugar's favourite of all the assignments. Plus, a lesson in exactly how it should have been done when Carina used her loaf to make a bread commercial. Watching along are Lord Sugar, Baroness Brady and Claude Littner. Rob and Gerald brave the red carpet at a celebrity hotspot in London to try to recover the unpaid wages of a former worker. Can they get him the large sum of money a tribunal awarded him?

programme.title: Bha mi Latha Samhriadh (When Stornoway Was Dancing)
programme.desc: A warm-hearted look back at aspects of the social life of Stornoway following the Second World War. It was a time of enormous change, with increasing wealth and a new culture imposing itself through music and film, but for a few halcyon years the baby-boomers uniquely enjoyed the best of both worlds

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Annabel MacLennan, currently a student of Celtic Studies at Glasgow University takes a trip through the centuries to meets some of the department's academics, past and present, and finds out about how the first Chair of Celtic came to be

programme.title: Fonn mo Bheatha
programme.desc: Cathy Ann MacPhee presents music and chat with guest Mairi Macinnes

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents a mix of hymns, psalms and faith stories. Glasgow Gaelic Musical Association perform Psalm 126 and Darren MacLean sings Faigh Flùraichean Bòidheach. Plus, the Rev Iain MacAskill shares the role faith plays in his life and Angela MacEachen reads from the Book of Proverbs

programme.title: Air an Smùid (Steaming)
programme.desc: A team of puffer enthusiasts battles to save the rusty, corroded VIC 27 from the scrap heap. On her rusty bow she bears the name Vital Spark, a throwback to her glory days as star of the BBC's 1994 series The Para Handy Tales. The team members look on nervously as she's carefully winched up the purpose built slipway. This is the first time in almost 30 years she's been out of the water, and at last they can assess the condition of her hull prior to ferrying her by canal to Crinan where her restoration begins

programme.title: Crodh Gàidhealach an Àird Bhàin: The Highland Cattle of Ardbhan

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Adam, Caroline, Sarah and Will, who have never met before, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: This summer, America has seen one of the most bitterly fought presidential election campaigns in living memory. As the President himself recovers from Coronavirus, voters reflect on the pandemic that has left America with the highest number of Covid-19-related deaths in the world, and the impact this has had on the economy. A summer of protests over racial justice has seemingly deepened the divide between Democrats and Republicans, and an increasingly ominous rhetoric around the validity of the election result could threaten the democratic process itself. So as Americans go to the polls, have they had enough their maverick leader or are they ready to vote him in for a second term? Can Democratic challenger and former Vice President Joe Biden make the case for a calmer, more traditional presidency that could begin to heal some of America's festering wounds? ITV's Senior International Correspondent John Irvine travels down the Mississippi River, from Minneapolis to St Louis, talking to A

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: This summer, America has seen one of the most bitterly fought presidential election campaigns in living memory. As the President himself recovers from Coronavirus, voters reflect on the pandemic that has left America with the highest number of Covid-19-related deaths in the world, and the impact this has had on the economy. A summer of protests over racial justice has seemingly deepened the divide between Democrats and Republicans, and an increasingly ominous rhetoric around the validity of the election result could threaten the democratic process itself. So as Americans go to the polls, have they had enough their maverick leader or are they ready to vote him in for a second term? Can Democratic challenger and former Vice President Joe Biden make the case for a calmer, more traditional presidency that could begin to heal some of America's festering wounds? ITV's Senior International Correspondent John Irvine travels down the Mississippi River, from Minneapolis to St Louis, talking to A

programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald

programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'The Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. As the case progresses, Barnaby becomes the thief's latest victim and it emerges that several high-society friends with connections to gangsters and a police chief have a few secrets worth killing for. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Jason Hughes, with guest appearances from Rik Mayall and Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Cassie and Sunny go in search of Hayley's family as the press begin to take an interest in the story. A detective who was involved in the original investigation offers his thoughts on why her disappearance remained unsolved, pointing out that Hayley's boyfriend was suspected of the crime but was never charged. The distance between where Hayley was last seen and where her body was found leads Cassie to form a new theory - that the killer may have travelled up from London, then returned before they could be questioned

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb

programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist publishes a book about his father's discovery of Celtic artefacts in an ancient mound, and plans a celebration on the site - but landowner Gareth Heldman is less than keen on the idea. While out walking, Joyce spots Heldman arguing with a woman, and hours later he winds up stabbed to death with an old weapon that bears a striking resemblance to the one found during the dig. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective is drawn into the strange murder of eminent art historian Professor Van Haren, who was really a former high-ranking Nazi hired by MI5 due to his expert knowledge of Soviet intelligence. Meanwhile, as Sam's husband Adam embarks on a busy career as an MP, she worries whether she will be able to share some big news with him. Crime drama set in the 1940s, starring Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks, with Tamzin Outhwaite and Rupert Vansittart

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Pete, Chris, James and Tim are all interviewed by Cassie and Sunny about the time they spent renting a holiday cottage near where Hayley disappeared over the turn of the millennium. James is the first to be called in, and reveals that Tim and Chris both had their families with them at the time. He recalls seeing press coverage of Hayley's disappearance, but never having seen her in person. Meanwhile, Chris is looking at flats with Jamila, who resolves that she needs to know everything about his past before she will take their relationship further

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Blaketon spots local coal merchant Arthur Bolton sharing a kiss with another woman and quickly gives the man's wife the bad news. However, the cheat is found murdered the next day and the former policeman becomes the prime suspect, leaving PC Walker to lead an investigation to clear his name. Peggy fails to back a winner when she buys a greyhound that refuses to run, and Bellamy's love life gets a much-needed boost

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Steve's engagement party gets underway. Alex makes advances towards Linda, and Biff rushes off to find her when Kim tells him she has left with Alex

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken is surprised when Denise turns up with Daniel. Vera insists the Armstrongs move back into the Rovers permanently. Stephen shocks Mike by cancelling his contract with him

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike cannot believe Stephen would cancel the contract over Don. Curly tells Andy that nothing happened between him and Anne. Denise tells Ken he could have regular access to Daniel

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the murder of a prize-winning author on the eve of the Midsomer Literary Festival. The event goes ahead as planned, but a killing occurs during a blackout at a packed reception hosted by the widow of another writer, and it is not long before the detectives uncover corruption, sexual scandal and financial mismanagement. Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After seven years of ducking the issue, Lewis and Hobson are embarking on a relationship, but the detective also has a new case to solve. Elderly don Richard Seager is murdered on the night of his release from prison, having served a sentence for causing death by dangerous driving. Crushed by a classic car, Seager mysteriously scratched the number 500 into its paintwork before his demise, and the academic's wife suspects a member of his victim's family is responsible for the killing. With Stephen Churchett, Alison Steadman and Edward Fox

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: It becomes clear to the detectives that the four men have conspired to come up with a false account of what took place the night Hayley disappeared. Jake is assigned to track down Tim Finch's ex-wife to hear her version of events - and uncovers disturbing information about Chris Lowe's past. On a personal front, Sunny is offered the opportunity to rekindle his past marriage, and Cassie worries about her father's failing memory

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.desc: Mr Roper decides he will not allow the flatmates to have a party. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy

programme.title: Man About the House

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine is disgusted with herself for sleeping with Curly, and he changes his mind about leaving. Liz visits Fraser, demanding to know what trouble Steve is in

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona thinks it is hysterical that Maxine slept with Curly. Andy and Jim are amazed when Liz reveals she has been talking to Fraser

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will is still angry with Tony, Marlon asks Kathy if he can experiment in the kitchen, and Sophie gets in a state when she thinks Zoe is going to propose

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie is shocked by Zoe's suggestion that they have a baby. Kim gets the better of Tara during a game of roulette. Tony waits for Will after school so he can talk to him

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A priceless Manet painting is stolen from a retired German art dealer's home, leaving Miller to suspect Lord Ashfordly's involvement. Peggy tries to convince a pair of visiting bird-watchers that Aidensfield's wildlife includes a rare bird of prey, and David tries to wing it as an ornithological expert. Rural drama, starring Rupert Vansittart, John Duttine, Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The flatmates crave a bit of summer warmth, and Mrs Roper's sunlamp mysteriously disappears. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are entranced when they meet Robin's older brother. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida's day goes from bad to worse when she suffers with a bad toothache, and Mrs Price pays a visit for tea

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker investigates when sibling rivalry and greed result in a fatal car crash. A devout couple are faced with money problems when their baby is diagnosed with a life-threatening condition. Meanwhile, Peggy is up to her usual tricks as she frightens some holiday-makers, and Jack and Gina get a scare of their own during a walk on the moors. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor, Murray Head and Tricia Penrose

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe has an early start to avoid talking to Sophie. Alan tries his best to locate Terry in Amsterdam. Kim gets Chris to admit that he is blackmailing her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jo tries to persuade Alan not to sell the pub. Ned tries to talk to Biff about his noisy love life. Paddy asks Linda if Mandy is seeing anybody

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Anne is excited about her first day as acting manager. Bill cannot forgive Maureen for sleeping with Curly, and Samantha tells Watts he cannot stay at number seven

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia thinks Jack wants her out of the Rovers, so she tries to win him round. Curly begins to regret returning to Weatherfield. Fiona makes plans to revamp the salon.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'The Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. As the case progresses, Barnaby becomes the thief's latest victim and it emerges that several high-society friends with connections to gangsters and a police chief have a few secrets worth killing for. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Jason Hughes, with guest appearances from Rik Mayall and Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Cassie and Sunny go in search of Hayley's family as the press begin to take an interest in the story. A detective who was involved in the original investigation offers his thoughts on why her disappearance remained unsolved, pointing out that Hayley's boyfriend was suspected of the crime but was never charged. The distance between where Hayley was last seen and where her body was found leads Cassie to form a new theory - that the killer may have travelled up from London, then returned before they could be questioned

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin is struggling to live off his student grant, so the girls find a way for him to earn hard cash. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The flatmates come up with a new rule to give their romantic pursuits a chance of privacy - but it means giving 24 hours' notice. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The Protectors hunt down a team of industrial spies. Crime drama, starring Robert Vaughn

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar has to find out whether a cool blonde is a secret agent or a crook - and if she could do him harm. Sixties spy adventure, directed by and starring Roger Moore

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A governess takes up a new position, but feels constrained to consult Holmes about some of the bizarre conditions stipulated by her employer. Period crime drama, guest starring Natasha Richardson, Joss Ackland and Patience Collier, with Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed is knocked out by gas while pursuing rogue agent Jimmy Merlin (Peter Barkworth). When he awakes London is deserted and under martial law, imposed by the embittered Brigadier Hansing (Joss Ackland), who has explosive plans. Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's brother-in-law Brian suffers a bout of depression, affecting everyone on the manor, and leading the dodgy dealer to make some dubious deals including giving one punter a £500 discount. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are trained to act as bodyguards to a Middle Eastern president who is preparing to sign an important peace treaty. Meanwhile, two killers are receiving similar training to prepare them to assassinate him. As their paths cross, the CI5 operatives learn the would-be killers have some useful inside information and share more in common with them than they may have imagined

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter uncover a plot to smuggle gold bars into the Middle East disguised as weights for an international strongman competition. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar plays his part in a gangster's plot to trick his four greatest enemies into carrying out a robbery. Stephanie Beacham features in an early role, alongside Roger Moore

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An interpreter claims to have been abducted by two villains who forced him to interrogate a badly bruised and obviously distressed Greek captive unable to speak English. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed helps Scotland Yard colleague Arthur Doyle on a murder inquiry with an abundance of clues, each one pointing to an illustrious suspect. However, all the clues prove fakes left behind by a blackmailer who is planting them to incriminate wealthy men as part of an extortion racket. Kenneth Cope guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A rival dealer schemes to put Arthur out of business, forcing him to advertise on pirate radio in a bid to stay afloat. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle's country holiday with their latest girlfriends spirals into chaos when they are held hostage in a farmhouse by two gunmen who have just botched a robbery. Adventure, with Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter investigate when a fellow officer is coerced into acting as a getaway driver by the gangsters who have kidnapped his wife. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman, and featuring a guest appearance by Billy Murray

programme.title: Cycling: La Vuelta a Espana Highlights 2020
programme.desc: Highlights of stage one. A shorter than planned rescheduled race began in the Basque Country town of Irun and finished at the top of the famous Arrate climb above Eibar. Gary Imlach presents, with Daniel Friebe reporting and Ned Boulting and David Millar on commentary

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Everyone's favourite idiot visits hospital to cure a nasty little problem, but proves to be a somewhat impatient outpatient, and later has trouble sleeping. Starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
